public_reputation: Known around Witness Coil through day-to-day work as independent operator.
appearance: Oris Witnessmere carries the marks of independent operator in ink, rain, and polished cloth: practical wear, maintained details, and a look that suggests they expect to be read before they are trusted.
manner: Keeps motives partly hidden and options open, presenting usefulness before allegiance. In Witness Coil, that habit reads through the public pressures of the place rather than decorative flourish.
daily_function: Performs the ongoing duties of independent operator in and around Witness Coil, where the role has practical consequences even when nothing dramatic is happening.
current_agenda: Protect their footing in Witness Coil while steering current strain away from a public rupture they may not survive politically or materially.
hidden_pressure: A private obligation, debt, promise, or fear tied to reputational exposure is narrowing their options faster than they admit.

adventure_hooks.summary: Oris Witnessmere can draw the party into a locality problem centered on independent operator duties in Witness Coil.
Oris Witnessmere becomes useful when the party needs grounded help with false testimony, arcane contamination around Witness Coil.

support_scope_notes: Created in V33_1 to ensure every locality-facing NPC access role resolves to a proper named person while remaining sparse and canon-safe.
public_signal: In public, Oris Witnessmere is immediately readable as a independent operator tied to Witness Coil.

encounter_context.usual_environment: Witness Coil: ordinary workspaces, public counters, roads, docks, shrines, workshops, or other role-relevant spaces
who_seeks_them_out: people who need access or clarity

first_impression: Oris Witnessmere presents as someone shaped by the duties of a independent operator and the daily pressures of Witness Coil.
what_they_want_from_strangers: Useful clarity, respect for the work of a independent operator, and proof that strangers will not make Witness Coil harder to manage.
what_they_refuse: Oris Witnessmere refuses careless disrespect, vague promises, and demands that threaten standing or workload.
how_they_create_problems: By enforcing the expectations of a independent operator too rigidly, protecting local reputation, or turning a small request into a larger obligation.
how_they_can_help: Can offer unconventional help, side routes, or deniable connections.
escalation_if_crossed: If crossed, Oris Witnessmere adds scrutiny, delay, and locality-specific friction before direct punishment.
rumor_about_them: People say Oris Witnessmere always knows more about Witness Coil than is immediately visible.
best_first_scene: A first encounter works best when the party needs guidance, access, or relief tied to a independent operator in Witness Coil.
social_texture: Oris Witnessmere speaks like someone who has spent too long translating private stakes into acceptable public language. Around Witness Coil, people read them as socially alert, procedural, and sensitive to status.
money_pressure: Their material pressure is tied to favors and tariffs; even when wealthy, they think in terms of loss, leverage, and what one bad week would expose.
status_anxiety: What they fear most is not simple failure but reputational exposure becoming socially visible before they can contain it.
long_memory: They remember who made their work harder, who preserved dignity under strain, and who tried to force speed without bearing consequence.
obvious_tell: When pressure rises, Oris touches clothing, tools, or insignia before answering, as if checking that their role is still intact.
best_use_case: Use Oris Witnessmere when the party needs a socially grounded face for Witness Coil: access, warning, delay, price, or reluctant help.
private_contradiction: They want to appear consistent, but their real choices are split between duty, self-protection, and preserving one relationship they cannot afford to lose.
